forecasts.discussion: At upper elevations, moderate danger exists due to wind transport of recent snow, creating slabs on leeward aspects where snow depths reach 76.6 inches. Cold content remains significant at -10, maintaining a dry snowpack. Middle and lower elevations show low danger with minimal new snow accumulation. Solar radiation is impacting southern aspects at lower elevations, where liquid water content has increased to 1.77, while northern aspects remain cold and stable with negligible liquid water.
forecasts.travel_advice: Avoid steep, wind-loaded slopes at upper elevations, particularly on east and south-facing aspects where snow depth is greatest. Best snow conditions are found on sheltered, northern aspects at middle elevations where the snowpack remains cold and surface hoar has not been capped by significant slabs.

forecasts.discussion: Moderate danger prevails at upper and middle elevations. Middle elevations harbor buried surface hoar up to 0.76mm, which may act as a failure plane for persistent slabs. Wind speeds of 10.1 mph at upper elevations are actively transporting snow, though transport rates remain low at 0.03. Southern aspects across all bands show liquid water presence, peaking at 0.65 at middle elevations due to solar gain, which may weaken surface bonds.
forecasts.travel_advice: Practice cautious route-finding on middle elevation slopes where buried surface hoar is present. Avoid steep southerly aspects during the afternoon as solar radiation increases liquid water content. Highest quality and safest snow is located on mid-elevation, low-angle northern aspects.

forecasts.primary_concern: Wet loose avalanches on southern aspects
forecasts.discussion: Avalanche danger is low for lower and middle elevations. Minimal new snow of 0.6 to 0.9 inches has fallen over the last 24 hours. The primary driver of instability is solar warming on southern aspects, where liquid water values have reached 1.35 at lower elevations. Wind transport is negligible at 0.01, and cold content is shallow, ranging from -1 to -4. Northern aspects remain dry and stable across the surveyed elevation bands.
forecasts.travel_advice: Travel is generally safe across most terrain. Avoid steep southern aspects during periods of direct sunlight to mitigate the risk of small wet loose slides. The most consistent and high-quality snow is found on northern aspects at middle elevations where liquid water content remains at zero.
